global.title: Trump on Tour - Reuters
global.summary: Reuters says U.S.-Iran clashes in the Gulf are straining a fragile ceasefire, while Washington awaits Tehran’s response to a temporary peace proposal. The report also notes the Strait of Hormuz remains heavily disrupted, keeping pressure on oil prices and the global economy.
global.whyItMatters: Conflict, inflation, and uncertainty shape the anxieties people bring to church; this is a key story for prayer and pastoral awareness.

global.title: 5 things to know for May 7: Job market, Pope Leo XIV, ceasefire, tariffs, volcano eruption - CNN
global.summary: CNN highlights several fresh developments, including Pope Leo XIV marking one year since his election and meeting U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio at the Vatican, alongside a deadly volcanic eruption in Indonesia and a U.S. court ruling against broad tariffs.
global.whyItMatters: This gives Kris a quick read on major global pressures while also noting a significant Vatican moment with wider Christian and diplomatic implications.

global.title: CNBC Daily Open: Leadership challenge swirls around Starmer - CNBC
global.summary: CNBC reports that political instability in Britain is drawing investor concern, while tensions in the Middle East continue after U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged fire in the Strait of Hormuz. The piece also points to how these conflicts may complicate upcoming U.S.-China talks.
global.whyItMatters: Political instability abroad can quickly affect markets, migration, and public mood at home, all of which influence congregational life.

canadian.title: The wonkiest day of the year - Politico
canadian.summary: Politico’s Canada Playbook says Ottawa is hosting a major policy gathering as Chrystia Freeland returns and premiers, policy leaders, and Indigenous voices engage current national issues. It also flags debate around child care deals and a proposed lawful access bill opposed by Apple over privacy concerns.
canadian.whyItMatters: Federal policy on privacy, child care, and Indigenous affairs affects families and public trust—important context for pastoral care and public prayer.

canadian.title: A Western Company Just Locked Up Greenland's Rarest Deposit—and Its Backers Have Washington Ties - thedeepdive.ca
canadian.summary: The Deep Dive’s roundup notes a Canadian pension angle in global market turmoil, saying CPP savings are being pressured by shifting energy politics tied to U.S. policy and the wider conflict environment.
canadian.whyItMatters: Economic volatility touches retirement security and household confidence, which often surfaces in pastoral conversations about fear and provision.

persecutedChurch.title: Jerusalem Christians not surprised by attack on nun as abuse becomes routine - The Times of Israel
persecutedChurch.summary: The Times of Israel reports that Christians in Jerusalem say harassment has become routine, with clergy describing daily spitting and abuse. The article says 61 physical attacks were recorded in 2025, reinforcing that the recent assault on a nun was not an isolated event.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a clear call to pray for the church in the Holy Land and to remember that everyday intimidation can deeply wound Christian witness.

persecutedChurch.title: Visiting Berlin, Sa’ar says whoever harms Christians in Israel ‘will be dealt with severely’ - The Times of Israel
persecutedChurch.summary: Following international outrage over recent attacks on Christians, Israel’s foreign minister said those who harm Christians will face severe consequences. The statement signals official recognition of the seriousness of the issue.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This may mark a meaningful response to anti-Christian violence and is worth watching for whether words lead to real protection.

leadership.title: Why This CHRO Says Caring Is the Key to Leadership - Forbes
leadership.summary: Forbes argues that effective leadership begins with the heart: caring, trust, empathy, vulnerability, and psychological safety. The piece frames these not as soft extras but as practical drivers of retention, engagement, and healthy culture.
leadership.whyItMatters: A pastor leads people, not just programs; this is a timely reminder that care and trust are central leadership disciplines.

goodNews.title: Emunah’s Quiet Heroes - The Jerusalem Post
goodNews.summary: The Jerusalem Post highlights children’s homes in Israel caring for vulnerable young people through trauma and war. Despite sirens, disrupted routines, and fear, staff and children are still honoring others, giving care packages, and planning joyful milestones like a Bar Mitzvah celebration.
goodNews.whyItMatters: It is a hopeful picture of steadfast love under pressure—useful for encouragement, prayer, and preaching about resilient service.
